Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a billing record for a customer's fruit purchase.

totalCarat.description: Total carat count (In Carat - Out Carat).

caratType.description: Type of carat (Small Carat or Big Carat).

dueAmount.description: Remaining amount to be paid (Total Amount - Paid Amount).

paidTo.description: Name of the person to whom the payment was made (Gopal Dada, Yuvraj Dada, Suyash Dada, Gaju Dada).

dateAndTime.description: Date and time when the bill was generated.

managerId.description: Reference to the Manager who created the bill. (Relationship: Manager 1:N Bill)

Owner.title: Owner
Owner.description: Represents the owner of the business.

definition.description: Stores owner profiles. Each document represents a single owner.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owner. This is usually the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores manager profiles. Each document represents a single manager.

params.name: managerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the manager. This is usually the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores bills created by managers. Includes denormalized 'managerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: managerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the manager who created the bill. This is usually the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bill.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability following the specified design principles. It leverages denormalization to ensure Authorization Independence, structural segregation for homogeneous security postures, and access modeling for consistent authorization patterns. The structure is designed to handle the app's core features, including manager billing, bill generation, automated messaging, and owner panel functionalities.

**Authorization Independence:** The `bills` subcollection under each `manager` document includes the `managerId` field. This denormalization is CRUCIAL. It allows security rules to validate that the manager creating/modifying the bill has the correct access without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `manager` document.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   `managers` and `owners` are in separate collections, reflecting different access requirements. This prevents mixing data with different security needs.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** `/managers/{managerId}/bills/{billId}` establishes clear ownership of bills by managers.

**QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):**
*   Secure `list` operations are supported by the path-based ownership model. Listing bills under a specific manager is secure because rules can easily verify the manager's authentication and ID against the path. Security rules will check that a manager is authenticated, and that the manager id in the `request.auth.uid` matches the managerId in the path.

**Invariants:**
*   The structure maintains ownership integrity (managerId).
